nodes.label: Global CLAUDE.md
nodes.description: File at ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md loaded automatically in every Claude Code session regardless of project. Contains: session-start protocol (brain find <project>), extraction guidelines (what/not to ingest), ingest workflow, full payload schema, confidence calibration table, critical pitfalls (slug rewrite, check-before-create, orphan nodes, re-ingestion semantics). Acts as the sole onboarding doc for any LLM discovering brAIn without prior conversation context.

nodes.label: Auto-update hook
nodes.description: PostToolUse hook on Write|Edit events, configured globally in ~/.claude/settings.json. After every file edit, runs brain_hook.sh which injects an additionalContext reminder into Claude's context: '[brAIn] <file> vient d'etre modifie. Si changement structurel → brain_find + brain_ingest.' Claude decides whether the change warrants a graph update. Non-blocking, no LLM cost — just a jq-based shell script outputting JSON with suppressOutput:true.

rels.evidence: The global CLAUDE.md is what makes the project context pattern automatic — without it, a fresh LLM has no instruction to check the graph at session start or to ingest structural changes.
The hook closes the feedback loop of the project context pattern: when Claude edits a file, it gets a nudge to update the graph, keeping the graph in sync with the project state across sessions.
The global CLAUDE.md is the primary interface between brAIn and any Claude Code session — it is what makes brAIn usable by a fresh LLM without prior context.
The auto-update hook is the automation layer of brAIn's project context pattern — it makes graph updates semi-automatic rather than requiring deliberate manual action.
